title: Informal Conjecture (diameter bound improvement)
statement_text: The extra factor $\sqrt{\log n}$ (compared with the random tree $T_n$) in the diameter bound $5\sqrt{n \log n}$ for the block tree $BT(R_n)$ could be replaced by any function tending to $\infty$.
context_text: Theorem 1.2 establishes that, for $R_n$ sampled uniformly from the connected graphs in a block-stable class, the block forest $BF(R_n)$ has diameter at most $5\sqrt{n \log n}$ whp. Random labelled trees have diameter of order $\sqrt{n}$, so a gap of $\sqrt{\log n}$ remains between the two bounds. The authors conjecture this gap can be closed down to an arbitrarily slowly growing function.
notes: Stated in prose immediately before Theorem 1.2: 'We conjecture that the extra factor √log n (compared with the random tree Tn) could be replaced by any function tending to ∞.' No labelled conjecture environment.
paper_title: Random graphs from a block-stable class
